chunk_id: mineru_book_en_搬书匠-3299-Swift Data Structure and Algorithms-2016-英文版_page_111_1
text: The structure accepts any type that conforms to the Compar ab le protocol. The single initializer allows you to optionally specify the sorting order and a list of starting values. The default sorting order is descending and the default starting values are an empty collection:

chunk_id: mineru_book_en_搬书匠-3299-Swift Data Structure and Algorithms-2016-英文版_page_111_6
text: The PriorityQueue conforms to sequence, collection, and IteratorProt ocol, so you can treat it like any other Swift sequence and collection:
page: book_en_搬书匠-3299-Swift Data Structure and Algorithms-2016-英文版_page_111

chunk_id: mineru_book_en_搬书匠-3299-Swift Data Structure and Algorithms-2016-英文版_page_111_7
text: extension PriorityQueue: IteratorProtocol { public typealias Element = T mutating public func next() -> Element? { return pop() } } extension PriorityQueue: Sequence { public typealias Iterator = PriorityQueue public func makeIterator() -> Iterator { return self } }
page: book_en_搬书匠-3299-Swift Data Structure and Algorithms-2016-英文版_page_111

chunk_id: mineru_book_en_搬书匠-3299-Swift Data Structure and Algorithms-2016-英文版_page_111_8
text: This allows you to use Swift standard library functions on a PriorityQueue and iterate through a PriorityQueue like this:
page: book_en_搬书匠-3299-Swift Data Structure and Algorithms-2016-英文版_page_111

chunk_id: mineru_book_en_搬书匠-3299-Swift Data Structure and Algorithms-2016-英文版_page_111_9
text: for x in priorityQueue { print(x) } // coldplay
